query: Angular lazy loading in production: route boundaries, preloading, and bundle trade-offs

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this Angular, RxJS, or NgRx prompt, so broader Angular question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: Angular Route Loading Strategies
competitors.selectionReason: This is the most direct official Angular guide for eager versus lazy routes, loadComponent or loadChildren decisions, and preloading trade-offs.
theirStrengths: It is the canonical Angular source for route loading strategy, lazy routes, and built-in preloading guidance.
theirGaps: It is docs-shaped and less compressed around the interview trade-off of route boundaries, preloading, and over-fragmentation.

realWorldUseCases:
Route-boundary decision

Lazy loading is not just "load later." In a production Angular app, it decides which routes stay out of the startup bundle, whether a feature should load with loadChildren or a standalone screen with loadComponent, and whether background preloading is worth the extra network work. The common failure mode is over-fragmenting features and turning one navigation into a chunk waterfall.
followUpConfusion:
Debug rule
  • Lazy loading helps startup only when the feature is genuinely deferred.
  • Too many tiny route chunks create network waterfalls and hurt perceived navigation speed.
  • Check route boundaries in DevTools before splitting more.

realWorldUseCases: The competitor covers the core concept, but our page is now more explicit about the production decision or failure mode behind this topic.
actionableExamples: The competitor still provides competitive examples, so the example bar remains tied instead of a clean sweep.
followUpConfusion: Our updated page now answers the likely follow-up confusion more directly with a sharper rule, boundary, or failure pattern.

actionableExamples: Keep one compact worked example near the top so the answer stays faster to scan than a broader reference.

competitors.winReason: The updated page now wins on production framing and follow-up confusion while the competitor remains competitive on raw examples.

competitors.label: Angular Standalone Components: Complete Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This Angular University guide is the strongest tutorial competitor for modern lazy-loading because it directly contrasts module-era and standalone route lazy loading.
theirStrengths: It gives practical standalone lazy-loading examples and explains how loadComponent reduces module ceremony.
theirGaps: It spends more time on standalone migration than on the narrower interview trade-off of preloading and over-splitting features.

competitors.label: Stack Overflow: How to think about Lazy Loading in Angular
competitors.selectionReason: This is a directly relevant community comparator for the mental model behind lazy loading, preloading, and when route splitting helps or hurts.
theirStrengths: It is directly about the mental model, which makes it a fair interview-style comparison.
theirGaps: It is shorter and less systematic about preloading strategy and feature-fragmentation trade-offs.
